payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Anyway. Last we left off, Vox Machina had confronted the Whispered One, the Undying King Vecna, in the Shadowfell. The battle did not go in your favor, with the death and very faint recovery of Vex, and the disintegration of Vax during this confrontation. Realizing that it was not going in your favor, you Plane Shifted to the Feywild, where you began to come up with some sort of plot or plan to try and bring your friend, Vax, back without a body. During this time Vax had a conversation with his patron, the Raven Queen, and was given an offer where he could return along with powers that would aid in this coming final confrontation. However, the deal came with some drawbacks, and upon his friends awakening the next morning in the Feywild, they found Vax returned to them, whole. Seemingly alive, returned, though missing his equipment. Equipment returned, and a brief period of figuring out what possibly delivered their friend back to this space. It was decided that based on his experience with his patron that the powers beyond the Divine Gate-- the Prime Gods that lay claim to the divine beyond the Prime Material plane will probably be the next best bet in gaining some sort of power, information about how to better prepare for the coming struggle with Vecna, the Whispered One. You then made your way to Vasselheim, where Pike went to her temple of Sarenrae which she had helped raise and reconstruct, and had a vision from her patron deity asking her to come visit her at the Island of Renewal, and was granted, through this vision, an object that would link, via your spell, a way to traverse past the Divine Gate and directly to her location on the plane of Elysium. Also, Grog and Scanlan had a little excursion in the city of Vasselheim and a minor altercation with the guards, which led to a larger altercation with the guards, which led to a much larger altercation with the guards, which led to them barely escaping and deciding it's time to go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, long walks on the beach.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He has more than one nipple, but less than five. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's an interesting tech night. Ash and I are Skyping in from the USCSS Nostromo, which is a Weyland-Yutani vessel and if we fart out it's because we've been eaten by xenomorphs. Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can't hear Ashley at all. Liam is totally overpowering. I love it

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (creepy voice) Samuel.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right guys! Well, that being the case, let's dive into tonight's episode of Critical Role! [dramatic music]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello, everyone, and welcome back. Hopefully with a fixed microphone?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll find out!

preceding_turns.speaker: CHRIS WILLMOTT
preceding_turns.text: We're good!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ah, perfect, awesome! Yeah, I love it! All right, so to get you guys get caught up to speed with what we did in recent episodes. (muffled dramatic music)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that you, Liam?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, so you guys want to take a short rest to do so?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I wanted two cheeseburgers. What did you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (in speak-and-spell voice) I am richer for having known you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you!  Much better!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said (robot noises). You said that that tower had, was full of some sort of flame, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and the, what little you can see, it seems to form this torso of a feminine structure, the body of which gives out these wings that curl upward. Held amongst the center of these wings is a large, gem-like flame. And you see within the actual crystalline-- we'll call it like a palace, if you will-- it looks like there's a light source within that flickers and dances like an eternal bonfire or flame, and it seems to have a pulse to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure this is unnecessary, but I'm going to start thinking about setting the Death Walker's Ward to fire damage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, so you guys want to take a short rest to do so?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I wanted two cheeseburgers. What did you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (in speak-and-spell voice) I am richer for having known you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you!  Much better!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What did you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said (robot noises). You said that that tower had, was full of some sort of flame, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and the, what little you can see, it seems to form this torso of a feminine structure, the body of which gives out these wings that curl upward. Held amongst the center of these wings is a large, gem-like flame. And you see within the actual crystalline-- we'll call it like a palace, if you will-- it looks like there's a light source within that flickers and dances like an eternal bonfire or flame, and it seems to have a pulse to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure this is unnecessary, but I'm going to start thinking about setting the Death Walker's Ward to fire damage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. So you take the better part of an hour, half an hour or so, 40 minutes or so, for a short rest to reset your armor to fire resist. Any other preparations?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, and the, what little you can see, it seems to form this torso of a feminine structure, the body of which gives out these wings that curl upward. Held amongst the center of these wings is a large, gem-like flame. And you see within the actual crystalline-- we'll call it like a palace, if you will-- it looks like there's a light source within that flickers and dances like an eternal bonfire or flame, and it seems to have a pulse to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sure this is unnecessary, but I'm going to start thinking about setting the Death Walker's Ward to fire damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you guys want to take a short rest to do so?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do we need a short rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's a 20-minute thing. Well, I don't have to, if everyone else wants to start.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're not in a rush, right? 20 minutes won't kill us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I guess. Sure.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You did take a long rest. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What does that mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Man. You're always a surprise. And after so many years.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I look at Pike. I say, Pike, this place is beautiful. It's incredible, and I don't think we would be here if it wasn't for you and your connection to Sarenrae. And I'm honored to be here with all of you all. And to see this place with Vax. This makes me happy. Just this moment. This is nice. (sighs) I love you all.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We love you, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We love you too, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I love you, Kiki.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just to be clear--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I use my inspiring leader feat. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was just about to ask, was that well-thought-out speech inspirational?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so level 18 that you are, with no charisma modifier, correct? Everyone gains 18 temporary hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we all healed up and healthy now?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yes, you can, during the short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We love you, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We love you too, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I love you, Kiki.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just to be clear--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I use my inspiring leader feat. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was just about to ask, was that well-thought-out speech inspirational?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so level 18 that you are, with no charisma modifier, correct? Everyone gains 18 temporary hit points.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Damn!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we all healed up and healthy now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You did take a long rest. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we did take a long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There were some healing spells spent on Pike's end and dealing with the eye that was recovered, I think. And then you with the Plane Shift.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me and Grog got shot a few times.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you got to heal a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But I can use some hit dice.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Pike, you lead on. You make your way through these tangled paths and they shift and curve and turn and the light never seems to change overhead and as you hike along and chat amongst yourselves and keep open to the vicinity around you and you're breathing in the clean air with hints of seaspray and saltiness that eventually makes its way through the breeze. You can feel parts of you sweat beneath your armor, from the general warmth of the atmosphere, and you travel for about an hour. Pike, and Vex and Vax and Keyleth, you pick up on this, too. The crystalline structure in the center of the island doesn't appear to be any closer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like people are avoiding us or does it look like this is not a place of--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. I had a funny feeling you would say that. 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see no signs of any movement, any foot tracks. No signs of anything in the vicinity that would lead you to believe that you're being avoided or being watched.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like anyone has been here for a very long time?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm not going to waste Luck on that. That's a one. I have no idea what's going in this fucking place.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, you watch. Pike, you walk for another ten minutes or so on your own, and you watch her travel, and as you focus, she's travelling, but she's still the same distance from everyone else she was when she left. She went just beyond eyeline and then it's weird. Like it doesn't make sense. You watched her maneuver and change and shift around things, yet she hasn't gotten any further or any closer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: See how many wristbands you can get.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll keep walking and I'll keep looking back, thumbs up, and I'll see what I can find.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You walk forward and you can look back at your friends and you continue to walk forward towards the structure. You eventually begin to weave between these stone obelisks, which get dense and overlap to the point where maybe about a hundred feet ahead, you lose sight of Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I want to get on my broom and fly up in the air and see if I can see her from the air.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You fly up in the air and you look down and you see her. She's right beyond where you lost sight of her originally, and she's walking forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay. How you doing, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Good, good. Just traveling over these stone structures.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. You join the rest of the group and you watch half your party still stumbling into pieces of stone. Pike, you with your eyes closed, still seeing this burning beacon, this circular sphere wreathed in flame that just licks up as the flames slowly burn out of the bottom of this structure and flicker off into nothing. You step for about two minutes or so in its direction, and the heat gets warmer and warmer and warmer, and then you open your eyes for a moment and you're right in front of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You all begin to close your eyes and begin walking and start wandering in different directions and bumping into pieces of stonework.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Bunch of cockroaches when you turn on the light.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike, you close your eyes for a moment and concentrate, and your vision is dark. You don't see the world around you, but within a few seconds of embracing this darkness, a sudden burst of light appears ahead of you, and you see a burning beacon visible in the direction where the crystal palace was before you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So I'll try keeping my eyes closed and start walking towards it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you guys are figuring yourself out, and you are concentrating so hard on being there--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn around. Is the tower any closer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it further?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shit. I walk back.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You sit there in silence for about a minute and a half as the darkness is there before you, pitch black. And you're clutching the symbol as hard as you can with your fingers, to the point where it's almost hurting your hand, and you see the faintest distant flicker.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Hey, guys, can you see me?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we hear her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys can hear her, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. I open my eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You look down and she's right in front of the crystal structure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She got all the fucking way there? What did you do, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I just thought about it. I closed my eyes and I started walking.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's what I fucking did.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I did, too. Ow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Open your eyes, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I walked the other way, and it definitely didn't work.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is she doing that we're not doing? Her hair is partially blue. Maybe we should dye our hair blue, as well.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Maybe you can only get here if you're a believer of Sarenrae.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I'm a believer. Can I try?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: (singing) I'm a believer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You close your eyes?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I'm going to clutch my symbol of Sarenrae and I'm going to close my eyes and I'm going to see if anything happens.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You concentrate. A minute. Two minutes. Five minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm there?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Sorry, not Grog. Percy was. You stayed back. I misremembered.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I grab Keyleth's hands.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Percival. That's my fault. So Percival, you made it past the boundary with Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How did you-- how did-- Shit. We should have followed Pike.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But Percy, you don't believe in anything. This is bullshit!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I believe in plenty of things. But, yeah, no, I agree. This is bullshit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to close my eyes and be like, Sarenrae, I really believe in you. You're so holy, and very divine and beautiful, and I think you do really good things for Pike, and I'm very happy about that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Another five, six minutes of darkness passes. Your eyes are closed. No beacon. Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Fuck. This is not working!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Pike. Pike, do you want to collect the rest of them and lead them back here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I scoop up Keyleth's hands and I say, you trust me, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, yeah, you know that good feeling that you have when you hang out with Pike? You know that warm feeling you have. Toasting marshmallows with her around the fire, that kind of feeling?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know that comes from Sarenrae, so focus on that with me. And we'll go to Pike. Focus on Sarenrae and that marshmallow feeling.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But that comes from Pike, not from Sarenrae.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know that's partially true, but not all true. Come on, give it a shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I try really hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Think about the marshmallowy goodness that is Sarenrae.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Vax and Keyleth do this. And then what do you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Close our eyes and show reverence to her.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. A minute. You emerge from the brush, and you walk and see Keyleth and Vax, eyes closed, clutching hands, just hoping. And Grog, looking bored as hell.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know that comes from Sarenrae, so focus on that with me. And we'll go to Pike. Focus on Sarenrae and that marshmallow feeling.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But that comes from Pike, not from Sarenrae.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You know that's partially true, but not all true. Come on, give it a shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I try really hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Think about the marshmallowy goodness that is Sarenrae.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Vax and Keyleth do this. And then what do you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Close our eyes and show reverence to her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Another five, six minutes of darkness passes. Your eyes are closed. No beacon. Nothing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. How long will it take me to walk back to them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you walk back to them?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I want to walk back to them.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. As you guys walk alongside, focusing on Pike and her leading this small pack of friends along through the heavily rolling hills and the pieces of stone obstruction that force you to make your way around. You help guide her around these various obstructions that get in the way. Pike, you're concentrating on this burning beacon of warmth ahead of you. And within three minutes of travel, it seemed almost closer than when you saw her disappear before. You guys look at her, and glance up and you've just come across the threshold of the stones, and you're now in this open sandy patch at the base of this large crystal structure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Do you walk back to them?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I want to walk back to them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. A minute. You emerge from the brush, and you walk and see Keyleth and Vax, eyes closed, clutching hands, just hoping. And Grog, looking bored as hell.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Where's Vex?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm up here, dear.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Come here. I have an idea. Okay? So everybody, hold onto me somehow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hold hands? Like preschool?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like a train?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Just don't let go. And I'm going to think real hard, and maybe we'll all be there together.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. We have to walk, though, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Eyes closed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right. I'm not closing my eyes. I'm just holding onto Pike's hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm still thinking about marshmallows, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thinking about bacon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I grab Pike by the earlobe.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That tickles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And Pike, do you close your eyes and follow that beacon once more?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: She looks down at Pike for a minute. "Until recently." And turns back to Scanlan and says, "And don't think your prayers have gone unheard, as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How do we seal away a god?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know. I was betrayed during the Calamity. I was not present when they began to seal the Betrayer Gods. You'd do best to speak with maybe some of the others who were more prominent in that time. You walk in Elysium. This is the realm also of Pelor."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The Dawnfather?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Might I ask, does Kord reside in Elysium?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, but you could perhaps speak with him, too. I don't know much about Kord. We don't see eye to eye." And if you could see a goddess glance sidelong with embarrassment, this is the closest you'd ever see. "I've fallen from import over recent years, until recently."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No. Holding her close-- and by this point, Sarenrae's form has expanded to almost the size of the crystal structure you came to. You can now see a parallel between her wings and her form and her torso as to the actual incredible presence of this god before you. And leans in, as she shows the island to Pike below and whispers close to your ear, "I'm not lonely here, Pike, for my sands remain with me, speak with me, and we learn from each other. Look." As she extends her arm, Pike, you look out, and all of the light of the sky seems to shine at once through every pearl, every grain of the beach sand that you've walked across. And where once you saw what was just a barren landscape, you now see thousands and thousands and thousands of people. Opaque. Translucent. Souls of those that have come to Sarenrae since the dawn of creation. Those who swore themselves to good under her banner and at the end of their lives came to become part of the Island of Renewal and forever live with her and amongst the rest of the community of people that are warmed under her light. They all seem to look up at once at you, and you sense them smiling, as the vision fades and the island goes back untouched. She drifts back down and places you on the ground. "You see, one day, you will join us, too. And we will all have so many stories to share." You now see her hovering over all of you as you step out of this to join Pike on the exterior of this tower. "While I am diminished, my powers limited, you do possibly face something terrible, unseen since the Calamity. And for this journey, Pike, I grant you my blessing." And as she reaches her hands out and they expand, her head lifts up, her eyes close, and her wings flare into a phoenix-like flame that just emblazons the sky with light and fire. The heat is more intense than anything you've felt, yet still there's no pain. It's just all-consuming. It's like the rebirth of a phoenix and you're all surrounded by it. For a moment, it's all there is in existence, just this incredible entity. And then the flames slowly die away, except for the flames that currently engulf Pike's gnome form. And as the flames burn away, you can see on Pike the familiar set of wings you've seen her spiritual form hold, which fold in and then dissipate. You now have the Blessing of the Everlight. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That would be quite helpful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It was hard getting here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Will he be as accepting as you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know. I haven't spoken much with the others in some time."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You should throw a party. Invite them all. Get to know them again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She looks down at Pike once more. "My Pike, you've done more than you know. You've been a guiding light for these wonderful champions and so many more." She reaches down and lifts you off the ground a bit, looking at your face, the bit of sadness that creeps across it as she lifts you up and you guys watch as she glides, passing through the crystal, Pike being brought below the archway as she just lifts her out beyond this crystal structure. She ascends upward about 25, 30 feet or so, holding Pike in her hands out in front of her. And just chucks her. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No. Holding her close-- and by this point, Sarenrae's form has expanded to almost the size of the crystal structure you came to. You can now see a parallel between her wings and her form and her torso as to the actual incredible presence of this god before you. And leans in, as she shows the island to Pike below and whispers close to your ear, "I'm not lonely here, Pike, for my sands remain with me, speak with me, and we learn from each other. Look." As she extends her arm, Pike, you look out, and all of the light of the sky seems to shine at once through every pearl, every grain of the beach sand that you've walked across. She reaches down and lifts you off the ground a bit, looking at your face, the bit of sadness that creeps across it as she lifts you up and you guys watch as she glides, passing through the crystal, Pike being brought below the archway as she just lifts her out beyond this crystal structure. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That would be quite helpful.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It was hard getting here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Will he be as accepting as you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't know. I haven't spoken much with the others in some time."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You should throw a party. Invite them all. Get to know them again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She looks down at Pike once more. "My Pike, you've done more than you know. You've been a guiding light for these wonderful champions and so many more." She reaches down and lifts you off the ground a bit, looking at your face, the bit of sadness that creeps across it as she lifts you up and you guys watch as she glides, passing through the crystal, Pike being brought below the archway as she just lifts her out beyond this crystal structure. She ascends upward about 25, 30 feet or so, holding Pike in her hands out in front of her. And just chucks her. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As a bonus action, you can summon the wings of the Everlight, granting you a flying speed of 40 feet for up to ten minutes. And while you have these wings, any spells you cast that cause creatures to regain hit points heal an additional ten hit points. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh my goodness! What is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get a plus-two bonus to your constitution.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll take a picture and text it to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: But once this ability is used, you can't use it for another seven days.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Oh my goodness! What is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You get a plus-two bonus to your constitution.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll take a picture and text it to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As a bonus action, you can summon the wings of the Everlight, granting you a flying speed of 40 feet for up to ten minutes. And while you have these wings, any spells you cast that cause creatures to regain hit points heal an additional ten hit points. (cheering)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, so you begin to leap up through the branches, and a lot of the trees here have winding and knotted branches, so it's pretty easy to find your way up the canopy after maybe a five minute climb. You get up and peek over and you can see now this orchard goes on for miles and miles, and miles, filling this vast valley that you can barely see mountains that crest the edge of it in the distance near the horizon. You also see there is no cloud in the sky, and there is no sun, but there is a single pillar of light that streaks down from way way up, the zenith striking down in the center of this orchard, where there you see before you this awe-inspiring sight of the Dawnfather's home: a gold-plated citadel of many humongous towers and spires that both resembles a military fortress and the largest-steepled church you've ever seen. It's hard to gauge distance with so many trees between you and it, you don't know if it's miles off from you, if it's maybe a thousand feet or so, but even at this distance, it's incredible in its scale.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I thought, but isn't that where the Dawnfather is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Island of Renewal is where Sarenrae is. The blessed fields of Elysium is the plane. He existed elsewhere in there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I understand now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is there the same ever-light sky?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ever-present, sorry. Ever-presence in light. I use my druidic die. 29. Sorry, 31.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's hard to see from down low, because the canopy does choke it a bit, you can see bits of the sky that appear blue, cloudless, whereas you saw wisps of cloud over the island, this appears clear, you can climb up a bit and try to get a better look if you want.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, actually, you guys walk for about three or four minutes in the direction of where you saw the fortress, and you come across what looks to be a small bush that's clustered near this dark wooden tree with a thick, gnarled exterior, and this bush has these small ripe plum-like fruits that come to a point at the bottom that you've never seen before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Souls don't grow on trees!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: At least he knows the dangers of monocultures. This is very impressive.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, this is, there's a lot of variety.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What're you guys doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Vax is off on his own, by the way, sitting on his ass having a moment, because it wasn't too long ago when he had a thing for Sarenrae, and that was pretty bittersweet so he's staring off into space.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: One piece of fruit? One piece, just one little piece. An apple.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're in Elysium! Oh my god, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If you do eat anything, and you shouldn't, but if you do eat anything, eat something you don't recognize, it'll be funnier that way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is there any fruit that I don't recognize?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (deadpan) No, don't, stop.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Something I don't recognize. 12.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, you walk on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nothing? Nothing. You want to check it for magic?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to check it. Hello, are you a soul?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I'm a soul.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Oh my god, it is a soul! It just spoke to me. Oh my god. What's your favourite color?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Blue, like your feather.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It said blue, like my feather! Did you hear it? It was talking, Grog. It was talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Don't eat it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Just in case!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What do I do with it? I already picked it!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have an idea! Why don't you hold onto it. Keep it in your pouch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not in the Bag of Holding, keep it in your shirt pocket.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When have I ever worn a shirt?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You can ask him if the fruit is edible, and make sure it's not people.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's fair. All right. I will hold onto the pointy plum.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we getting any closer to the citadel?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys keep walking towards it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Actually, Vax, Vex, and Keyleth, you notice fairly quickly in that you begin to see movement ahead. The air itself, which by the way, the wind here is carrying a very sweet and fragrant, almost like honey flower whiffs that come through in waves that hit you, the whole atmosphere here makes you hungry, it makes you comfortable, it reminds you of childhood in a strange way. It's a very good, positive atmosphere. You begin to see as you approach, figures, moving. Pike, you recognize this because these are similar to the vision you just had from Sarenrae. They are entities. They are souls. They are people that walk the fields of Elysium, and you see them walk with small satchels, and you see them picking the fruit. And gathering, and harvesting. They don't seem to pay you too much mind, and as you begin to get close to some of them, they just stop and watch you. It's very Spirited Away, in a strange way. You see these somewhat translucent entities that barely acknowledge your existence and then as you pass by, they go on to whatever work they were doing. No speech, you don't even see eyes or faces, you just see the outline of the human-like shape, various heights and sizes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I'm a soul.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Oh my god, it is a soul! It just spoke to me. Oh my god. What's your favourite color?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Blue, like your feather.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It said blue, like my feather! Did you hear it? It was talking, Grog. It was talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hey, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Don't eat it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Just in case!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What do I do with it? I already picked it!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have an idea! Why don't you hold onto it. Keep it in your pouch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not in the Bag of Holding, keep it in your shirt pocket.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: When have I ever worn a shirt?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You can ask him if the fruit is edible, and make sure it's not people.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's fair. All right. I will hold onto the pointy plum.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we getting any closer to the citadel?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys keep walking towards it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you walk on.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there any people? Does it look like there's tracks in this orchard?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Because you have no sun to keep tabs on, you're just going to have to fly up above the canopy and keep an eye on it, so you're cresting and descending through the orchard as you progress. You travel for about an hour and a half to two hours, and you notice you are getting closer, but it is a long way away, you probably have another three, four hours of travel before you get there on foot.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: One of you two. Is this technically favored terrain for you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It would count as a forest for you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah? I guess I'll be in the front. Percy, have you done a lot of talking to Pelor?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Not really, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Percy, how do you feel about gods these days? Last time we talked we were both not optimistic, I feel like.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I know how to be polite. I'm very reasonable at that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Reverence.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Reverence.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You can certainly acknowledge when someone can squash you like a bug.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But a lot of things can squish us like bugs. I don't know, this is complicated. Let's just keep going. I'm going to keep an eye out while we're walking, for anything that resembles the Sun Tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right. Are you leading the way?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And as you push closer and closer to where this brightness continues to expand, and it's brighter than you anticipated. You're getting to the edge of a clearing where the tree line comes to a halt, but as you do, it hurts your eyes a little bit. You have to adjust from the dark interior of the forest to the outside. And as you finally breach the edge of the tree line and look out, you can see this open clearing, and immediately you can see the column of light that's cracking down from the center of the sky is what is lining this clearing; where the light touches, the trees stop. And it hurts a little bit to look at it because the light is reflecting off of this bright gold brass-colored citadel before you. You thought the crystal structure that Sarenrae was in was, you know, impressive. This is four times larger than the largest structures in Vasselheim or Emon you've ever encountered. And you watch as this massive citadel-- It has maybe two dozen separate towers of different heights that stagger and shift and they all come to these steeple-like points. And there in the center of the structure is one thick, massive tower that crawls upward and upward and upward and upward for, even at this point, surmising 15, 20 stories before the tip vanishes into the bright light that is descending from the sky above. You see the outline of a few sparse trees that catch your attention. You do see some trees that litter this open space within this bright light that are of the same type of tree as the Sun Tree that resides in the center of Whitestone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See, this tree can squash me like a bug. If it fell on me, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I wonder if its apple grew as big as the actual tree. If it fell, you'd be doomed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you progress, a few more hours into this trek, eventually, Vex, you begin to notice the light ahead begins to grow brighter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Hey, I think we're getting closer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Awesome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does this feel like sunlight? Does it register like sunlight or is it a whiter light?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like should we put some sunblock on as we walk towards it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah? Is it sunlight temperature?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It registers as a sunlight. It is a pure light.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Like kitchen light white or like reading room light?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Like noon daylight light.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Arizona or California?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you begin to get closer and closer to the citadel, once again the scale seems to build and build. What should seem to be a shorter walk gets longer and longer as you begin to see how massive and far away this is, compared to where you thought it was. And about 30 minutes since you left the treeline, you begin to just get past the final bit of Sun Trees that are planted around the exterior.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should've worn sunglasses here.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Soft music you hear playing throughout the interior of the fortress, like this distant sound of a thousand soft choir voices accompanying the inspirational sight of the building that you now walk through. You see other entities adorned in armor, still opaque and pearlescent in their spirit form. Some of them have flowing robes of gold and white that trail behind them and drift across the floor silently. The further in you walk, you begin to see where some of these spires and spiral staircases twist upward. The rooms have purpose in construction, but the scale of those that walk through the halls is so varied that you see staircases on large scales and small scales. They almost seem to wrap around each other in places. It's surreal, almost Escher-esque in some of the interior construction. You see archways and doorways on different levels: some that appear to be reached only by those that can fly versus those that can just walk and climb. Occasionally, you look up and you can see skylights across the top: some open glass and crystal, some appearing to be a small window of stained glass that glitters down in bright blues, purples, reds, oranges. There's definitely a stained glass motif in a lot of the interior of the fortress, which you've seen in some of Pelor's imagery, and the symbol around him is like a circular stained glass structure that spirals in with the actual star and the sun in the center. The music continues to grow louder and louder the further in you walk, and you feel like you're being led along for a good 15 minutes straight. This structure, this hallway, just goes on and on, the arches passing one after the other after the other. Your eyes have adjusted to the brightness of the interior now. Eventually, you come to another set of large doors. These doors themselves: no wood, no stone, just pure, vibrant platinum and gold. The symbol upon them seems to be the same circular motif as the symbol of the Dawnfather, with the star in the center, but the detail and intricacies. You get close, and your eyes-- especially you, Vex and Vax-- you glance and you can see, on every small scale, even just a simple bar of the star and the sun has writing, and has artwork, and then as you look closer, even on a smaller scale, that writing is comprised of smaller artwork and smaller writing. You're thrown off by the level of detail and intricacy of this place's construction. You're in awe. Your jaw drops as these reveals seem to barrage you with each step into the structure. At that point, the doors (scraping noise) open, and you wince once again, as the light that fills the room from the inside is near-blinding. You close your eyes a bit, and all you hear is this deep, resonant voice say, "Come."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I beg to differ.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The outside's a different story.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go to the courtyard.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just you wait!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No shadow underneath us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No shadow underneath you. Looking around you, and looking at your hands, you can see very faint shadows can be cast between your fingers and things that are very close, but it's like there's a diffused light throughout the entirety of the interior of the fortress that casts away any darkness or shadow. There's no umbra; there's no place where light is absent. It is just light everywhere. You hear this soft music played, the deeper into the fortress you get.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Club's over here, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Rocks fall; everybody dies. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Benny Hill music commences.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: No, the light's coming from the center of the room. As your eyes slowly adjust and you glance past your fingers and your hands, you see there in the center of the chamber is a 25-foot throne of gold, brass, silver, and ruby. Sitting upon that throne sits a mighty being, wearing the most intricate and beautiful golden plate armor you've ever seen. Similar in detail and design as the interior of the chamber around you, it almost blends in if you didn't see it shift. A white cloak encases the shoulders of this entity and sways gently at their side like it's been brushed off and dangles over one of the arm rests. And where a head would be, instead there is a burning star, so bright that you can only look but a second before you have to avert your gaze and shut your lids out of the pain. Just enough time to barely make out the outline of what appears to be a male head, almost featureless, burning within the corona. This, you can only imagine, is the Dawnfather himself, Pelor. As you step into the chamber, the door (boom) closes behind you, and the winged planar entities that pushed you in are not in the chamber. It's just you... and the Dawnfather.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is the light coming from the tower?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Another seeks the path of the Matron of Ravens. A flaw in our annihilation of her hubris. That it still exists is dire news. That it exists in the hands of such a slippery, vile mortal as Vecna, even more so. I thank you for your haste in sharing your findings. I shall ponder a strategy." He leans back in his chair, brushes his hand like this, and the doors behind you (boom) open.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Next time we visit the Dawnfather, wear some underwear. This floor is like a mirror.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: But I don't ever-- oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What are you-- oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh! You mean I can-- oh, sorry. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Vox Machina."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Little bit of pee comes out. A little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You guys, he knows our name.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You come with purpose. I can sense your determination. What would you tell me of your purpose?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Vecna walks the earth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We are here to prevent the coming of a third Calamity.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We seek the means to destroy him and all he stands for.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He seeks ascension into godhood.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And all your children are in danger, and we have begun communing with the gods. I extend slowly the wings and bow my head in deference to him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The Divergence is threatened. Your guards, your creatures, have some of our proof in their care, at the moment.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I could destroy this right now, only if you do not need it. Its existence is a threat. But the choice is yours, since you brought it before me."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Only if it can help us destroy Vecna.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you know how this would help you destroy Vecna?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We don't know how to destroy Vecna.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We seek knowledge and power.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And assistance.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The beacon comes back to you again. The stage beneath you, everyone around you, seems to vanish in the light that's the attention of this entity, this god, ancient beyond all thought and reason that you would contemplate, awaiting your answer.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you know what help it would be to us on the Material Plane?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He stares through you. The beacon finds you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's not the only one in existence. There are others.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think it might be, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's his eye.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There are other people missing eyes, but this is the only one--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's his. And it should be destroyed.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe it'll make him weaker, if it is destroyed. He obviously put it in his scion. We destroy it, we keep someone else like Delilah Briarwood from coming into power. Right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I think...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I say destroy it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Delilah was obviously his pawn and she was using it. Fuck it. Destroy it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We stand with... a master of the Grey Hunt. She is the one who can speak for Whitestone on that level. If you say destroy it, we destroy it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 19? 17. so you slam into the wall and you hear (groans) behind you. The stained glass that shattered, reforms right where you left it, and you continue to ascend upwards. Still holding onto you, but it's now disadvantaged on its next roll because you slammed it into the wall. So (flapping) making your ascent upward as fast as you can, you watch as the tower slowly pincers closer and closer. You watch as another layer begins to close above. All right. He's going to attempt to try and slam you into the wall again. Go ahead and make another athletics check. Roll a d20 and add your strength modifier. Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He's on my back, or he's like hanging onto me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's on your neck and head. He's holding on and trying to steer you off to the side by controlling your movement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to spin and try to slam into the wall and knock him into the stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can she do like a wing thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to use my wings to thrust me faster into the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, go ahead and make an athletics check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, no. Okay. Athletics check, was a plus seven, so 19.

payload.raw_text: As you exit the chamber, you can see the various souls and planar that wander the halls on their own business have all now joined shoulder to shoulder as a procession, showing you a respectful exit from the compound. As you walk, they all seem to give a nod of respect towards Vex'ahlia, the now freshly chosen champion of Pelor for this endeavor. As you step beyond the staircase that leads you down beyond the Fortress of the Sun, into the center of the clearing that is watched and protected by the various trees of the Dawnfather, looking out amongst the endless progression of the orchards, you consider what your next path is to discover who and what this key is to Ioun, and hopefully there's enough time., because time, right now, and the decisions you make, will greatly affect what can and cannot be done. And that's where we're going to end tonight's session.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Shit. Is there a hole in it? No?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: No hole. Sarenrae was not going to teleport you across the realm, letting you take away one of the prized souls of her people.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: That was the pope, Travis. That was the pope.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Oh man. You should really not change that crest, because it's in the campaign guide that's being published in two months.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. What the fuck!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Champions over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) We are the champions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is so fun. I haven't been able to do epic-level content! That's so fun!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is every episode going to be this emotional for the rest of the game? I can't keep doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Liam, what did you say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I said the twins are officially salt and pepper shakers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You totally are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, and I have the white--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have the white dragon scale armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's actually pretty great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think this actually changes the crest of Whitestone now, because now there's a sixth champion of Pelor who's of Whitestone. Six stars, not five now. Holy cow.
